violations.text: Garlic, pooled eggs, and tofu were observed held at between 59-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved items to another cooler unit to actively cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
Prep unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (ice buildup w/ ambient of 53F. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed/sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Eggrolls and chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Chicken AND dumplings were observed held between 47-69 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, items were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
Various items were observed stored on the floor in the freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
Lowboy located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (ambient of 50F). Unit MUST be able to maintain TCS items at 41F or lower. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The ceiling tiles in the back kitchen area is in poor repair (broken tiles and previous water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Various items were observed held between 45-63 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in walk-in cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed noodles cooling in a container greater than 4 inches deep in the walk-in cooler. PIC placed in shallower containers. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Various items were observed stored on the floor in the freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed and washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The lowboy located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Inspector observed an ambient temperature of 70 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit must be able to maintain TCS items at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. PIC relocated TCS items to walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - cooking equipment - prep unit - refrigeration units Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The ceiling tiles in the kitchen storage area is in poor repair (previous water damage and broken tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC disposed of ice and took out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Pork was observed held at 107 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Various items were observed held between 46-114 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items in walk-in cooler and disposed of garlic w/ oil. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked chicken was observed cooling on the counter. PIC placed in walk-in cooler. Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, items were placed warm in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed in shallower containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Shrimp was observed thawing in the 3 bay sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: - under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); - completely submerged under running water; - as part of the cooking process; - or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen, walk-in cooler, and walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Utensils (rice spoons) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed and wash/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Inspector observed clean food containers stored on the floor in the back kitchen area. PIC placed at dish machine to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Inspector observed various single-service items stored on the floor in the back hallway. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
Rice cooker located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Observed handle wrapped in tape. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Cardboard is used in the construction of shelf liners in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris:- cooking equipment,- hood vent,- prep unit,- refrigeration units,- soda syrup dispenser in the back kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
The ceiling tiles in the back kitchen area is in poor repair (previous water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
All handwashing sink are lacking signage. A sign or poster that notifies FOOD EMPLOYEES to wash their hands shall be provided at all HANDWASHING SINKS. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Shrimp (in a bucket) was observed thawing in a mop sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing.PIC informed to cease practice. Informed of the proper thawing procedures. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
Boxes and buckets of food product were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
Rice cooker located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Observed handle wrapped in tape. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces have accumulation of debris: 1.shelves throughout kitchen observed with food and soil debris. 2.bakers rack have an accumulation of soil and food buildup. 3.prep units observed with food residue and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
All handwashing sinks are lacking signage. A sign or poster that notifies FOOD EMPLOYEES to wash their hands shall be provided at all HANDWASHING SINKS. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice disposed. Unit taken out of service to be wash and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Various food product was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Working containers of dry foods are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen area(behind equipment). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to keep current PCS and send proof of services to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Boxes of food product and vegetables were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler, walk in freezer, and food storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry food product. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Inspector observed equipment(pans and bowls)be stored on wood plank on floor in kitchen area.PIC instructed to wash, rinse, and sanitize equipment and store properly on a clean surface. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. PIC informed and disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Fried rice cooker located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Observed handle wrapped in tape. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces observed with debris: 1.shelves throughout kitchen observed with food and soil debris. 2.walls and food storage shelves in walk in cooler observed with food residue, soil residue and other debris. 3.all refrigeration units(including gaskets) observed with food residue and other debris. 4.hood/ventilation have an accumulation of grease buildup and other debris. 5.bakers rack observed with an accumulation of soil and food debris. 4. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The floors and walls throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue ,food debris and/or grease. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
